title: Stacked Line Chart to isolate values instead of summing them
excerpt: I want to create a stacked line chart that will show how each item is performing over time: the first item shows the true numerical value month to month, but the rest of the items then add up the values even when “cumula…

posts.author: tmauney (Taylor Mauney)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to create a stacked line chart that will show how each item is performing over time: the first item shows the true numerical value month to month, but the rest of the items then add up the values even when “cumulative data” is unchecked. When I hover over the plot point, it lists the true value, but visually I’m not able to see how the items are performing in comparison to each other over time because it calculates the sum of all items’ numbers.

numbers formula chart dashboards

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you need clean, non-stacked trend lines that actually reflect each item’s performance over time, you might consider Time in Status for monday.com. Its reporting board includes line and bar charts that use the real underlying values (not stacked totals), so you can compare items or categories over time without the distortion you’re seeing in monday’s widget.

If helpful, here’s the chart view reference
